The whitespace
No platform owns ParentTech + CareerTech + HRTech + EdTech together — until now.

Each category is mature and well-funded on its own, but every player in it stops exactly at that category's edge. A parenting app tracks a child's growth with no link to their resume a decade later. A career tool builds a job-ready profile from scratch, with no access to the person's real history. A hiring platform recruits off that resume with no way to verify any of it. A school certifies learning, then hands the student back with no bridge to the job market at all. No single company has ever owned a person across all four stages at once.

Parents

Real wins vanish. A 5th-grade quiz win, a school football title — nothing captures them, so by resume time they're simply gone.

Candidates

Building a career story is rare and high-stakes. Most people get little real practice and no honest coach telling them what's actually working.

Companies

Every resume reads the same. Recruiters have no fast, trustworthy signal for who genuinely fits the role in front of them.

Faculty

Exams are still built and graded by hand, right when faculty have the least time to spare — with no fast view of where the class is struggling.

The opportunity

A market already worth billions — before it even has a name for what SKOAR is building.

Four sourced categories, each sizable on its own. Figures below are third-party research estimates, cited per card — directional, not audited.

EdTech · India $0.0B → projected $29–30B by 2030–31 IBEF · IMARC Group
HR tech · India $0.0B → projected $2.3B by 2034 IMARC Group
Private tutoring · India $0.0B → projected $8.2B by 2034 IMARC Group
Higher-ed students 0.0M 45,473 colleges · 1,168 universities AISHE 2022-23
K-12 students 0M 1.47M schools nationwide UDISE+ 2023-24
Registered MSMEs · India 0.0M SKOAR's exact recruiter segment — 1 to 50-employee firms IBEF · Udyam registrations, Feb 2026
↳ ParentTech — child achievement tracking — has no established category size in market research yet. SKOAR isn't entering a sized category on the parent side; it's positioned to define one.
Ranges vary meaningfully by research firm and publish date — treat every figure here as directional pending independent verification.

One score. A whole journey.

SKOAR follows achievement across a lifetime — from a child's first medal to a college exam to the day they're hired. Four portals, four AI companions, one continuous profile.

Parents
Candidates
Companies
Faculty
The idea

One profile, built over an entire lifetime.

SKOAR captures achievement at each life stage instead of asking people to reconstruct it later — and lets one verified profile move forward with them.

01

Parents

Record every win, milestone and certificate, from school to national level.

02

Candidates

Turn a lifetime of achievement into a scored, job-ready profile.

03

Companies

Rank applicants on real substance and hire straight from the pipeline.

↔ parallel track

Colleges run their examinations on the same intelligent rails — and a strong exam result can flow straight into a student's own candidate profile.

The moat

Four AI companions. One family.

Every portal has a conversational agent built for its user. They stream answers in real time, and never invent a number — every figure they say out loud traces back to a real, server-side computation.

Hunter

AI career intelligence

A job-search copilot that reads your real resume, explains your SKOAR Score, and finds roles that actually fit — all in plain conversation.

→
Resume intelligenceScores and rewrites weak sections into stronger bullets.
→

Rank & shortlistRanks applicants on role fit and custom criteria.

For parents

Keeper

AI parent guide

A warm, non-competitive guide that makes sense of a child's real achievements and suggests meaningful next steps — never a ranking against other kids.

→
Understand progressExplains strengths and areas to grow, in plain language.
→
Suggest next stepsRecommends activities and mentorship programs that fit.
→
Always supportiveJudgment-free guidance, grounded only in real data.

For faculty

Examiner

AI faculty assistant

Drafts exams from uploaded course books by conversation, then answers class-performance questions with bounded, verifiable stats — nothing ships without a human review.

→
Create exams by chat"25 questions, difficulty 7" becomes a draft in seconds.
→
Class insightsMiss-rates, score distribution and weak topics, on request.
→
Verify & trustEvery number is a real server-side calculation.
✓ No agent generates, publishes, or releases a grade without an explicit human confirmation step.
Product

Four portals, feature by feature.

One shared design system and backend, four purpose-built experiences.

The SKOAR ScoreA gauge, grade badge, percentile and full breakdown of what's driving it.
AI ranking engineReads your real resume content and scores it against the market.
Resume fixerAI rewrites weak sections into stronger, quantified bullets.
Job-description matchPaste a JD and see exactly where you fit and fall short.
Jobs boardBrowse open roles — your resume text travels with the application.
Interview prep & debriefTailored practice and a prioritized, concrete action list.
Recruiting dashboardStat cards with week-over-week deltas, adaptive to activity.
Applicant pipeline & funnelApplied to hired, with conversion tracked at every stage.
Attention queueStale applications and awaiting-decision candidates, surfaced.
Cached insightsTop-candidate and slow-response callouts, AI-phrased, dismissible.
Time-to-hire trackingCompare similar roles and candidate-quality trends over time.
AI interviewsSend AI-run interviews to candidates and review completions.
Achievement VaultEvery award, tagged by level from school to international.
Document LockerCertificates kept private, retrieved only via signed links.
Growth TimelineVault items, milestones and mentorship in one chronological story.
Mentorship programsBrowse age-matched programs and apply on your child's behalf.
Guardian accessInvite a co-guardian by link, with role-based permissions.
Multiple childrenA quick switcher — every child gets their own vault and timeline.
Courses & booksUpload a book; it's parsed into chapters automatically.
Two ways to build an examA precise form, or a conversation with Examiner.
Review, edit & publishEvery draft passes a human review screen before students see it.
Anti-cheat signalsTab-switch, blur, paste and originality flags, captured live.
AI grading + overrideShort answers auto-graded, then reviewed by faculty before release.
Class performance insightsNEWMiss-rate questions, score splits, and declining-student alerts.
Business model

Six segments. Each priced for how it's actually used.

Episodic use gets credits. Steady use gets a subscription. Institutions get an annual license. The model follows the behavior, not the other way around.

Individual

Candidates

Credit-based

Free tier, then Starter / Growth / Pro packs — pay for resume polish, mock interviews and AI generation as you use them.

CycleTop-up
Business

Companies

Subscription

Tiered by hiring volume — active jobs, AI interviews included per month, and full pipeline analytics at the top tier.

CycleMonthly
Individual / family

Parents

One-time + credits

One-time family unlock for Vault, Milestones and Timeline. Keeper AI conversations sold in credit packs on top.

CycleOne-time + top-up
Individual

College faculty

Credit-based

Semester credit packs covering exam generation and short-answer AI grading — matched to the academic term.

CyclePer semester
Individual

Tuition teachers

Credit-based

Lower-priced monthly or per-batch packs, using the same classroom roster and student-ID delivery as schools.

CycleMonthly / per batch
Institution

Schools

Annual license

Flat, tiered by enrollment, with a generous usage allowance — no per-exam metering the school has to track.

CycleAnnual
↳ Schools and tuition teachers share one delivery mechanism: a roster-generated student ID, handed to the parent, who links it in their own portal. Students never need an account — which is what makes classroom adoption frictionless, and it's the same infrastructure serving two different pricing motions.
Under the hood

Built to be trusted with real records.

One workspace of React apps over a shared Node backend and a single Firebase project — with strict, per-user data boundaries enforced server-side.

Safety by design

Agents never fabricate numbers — every figure is computed server-side and only phrased by AI.

Privacy boundaries

Ownership is checked on every request — no user ever sees another's data through a clever question.

Human in the loop

No exam is generated or published, and no grade released, without explicit confirmation.

React + Vite + TypeScript Tailwind CSS v4 Node.js + Express Firebase Auth & Firestore Cloud Run · asia-south1 Claude Haiku — chat Claude Sonnet — generation & grading SSE streaming
SKOAR

A profile that starts at a school prize-giving and ends in an offer letter.

Four agents, four portals, one continuous record of what a person can actually do — built on SKOAR.

SKOAR — India's AI-native HR & education platform
